name: Nick Banks Consulting Design System
description: Visual and editorial system for Nick Banks Consulting (NBC). A practical leadership support practice that sits between fresh/AI-fluent and grounded/human. The system has to hold both.

brand.name: Nick Banks Consulting
brand.longTagline: Practical leadership support for organizations doing work that matters.

positioning.essence: NBC helps organizations make important work clearer, more human, and more executable.
audience: Mission-driven leadership teams in healthcare, manufacturing, housing, hospitality, and creative sectors
Younger founder-led companies (30-something founders, scaling teams, modern operators)
Boards and senior leaders looking for strategic clarity and leadership development
positioning.tension: Fresh, current, AI-fluent, build-first AND grounded, human, steady, care-led. The system has to hold both. Most consultants pick one.
isNot: Not a warm cream-led editorial brand (earlier explorations used cream as a primary surface; the brand now uses pure white as default with off-white reserved for documents)
Not a corporate consultancy aesthetic (no chevrons, drop shadows, stock photography)
Not a startup SaaS aesthetic (no purple gradients, glass-morphism, AI swoosh graphics)
Not a coach/personal-development aesthetic (no soft pastels, no 'unlock your potential' register)

white.description: Default surface for digital applications - web, decks on screen, social cards. The brand defaults light.

ink.description: Contrast/dark variant of artifacts. Used for decks projected in dim rooms, contrast sections within light-led web pages, and any artifact that calls for the dark treatment.

offWhite.description: Document-only light surface for proposals, contracts, letterhead. Never used in digital surfaces - those use white.

redFullBleed.description: Red as a full surface. Used once per artifact, typically the closing slide or hero CTA. The graphic crescendo.

primary.description: Display headlines on white and off-white surfaces.

body.description: Body type on white and off-white surfaces.

muted.description: Captions and secondary labels on white and off-white surfaces.

primary.description: Headlines and primary type on ink surfaces (contrast variant).

secondary.description: Captions, secondary labels on ink surfaces (contrast variant).

red.description: The signature red. Used as: the dot, monospace label color, red rules, full-bleed accent surfaces. Punctuation, never paint, except for designated full-bleed surfaces.

onLight.description: Brand red at 35% opacity. Used for the racing number motif on light surfaces. Same red, calibrated to recede on white.

onDark.description: Brand red at 25% opacity. Used for the racing number motif on ink surfaces. Same red, calibrated to recede on dark.

hairlineLight.description: Hairline rules on white and off-white surfaces.

rules: White (#FFFFFF) is the default surface for everything digital - web, decks on screen, social.
Ink (#0E0E0F) is the contrast variant. Used for decks shown in dim projection environments, dark sections within light-led pages, and artifacts that call for it.
Off-white (#F5F1E8) is for documents only - proposals, contracts, letterhead. Never use off-white in decks or on the web.
Red is punctuation, never paint, except for one designated full-bleed surface per artifact.
Racing numbers use the brand red at reduced opacity (35% on light, 25% on dark), not a separate muted color.
Don't add colors outside this palette.
Don't use gradients, shadows, glows, or decorative effects.
All hairline rules are 0.5px.

display.name: Inter Black
display.use: Display headlines, section breaks, brand wordmark

labelMedium.name: Inter Medium
labelMedium.use: Eyebrow labels, small caps, the 'CONSULTING' descriptor on the wordmark

mono.name: JetBrains Mono
mono.use: Labels, captions, '// comments', page numbers, tags. The AI-fluent signal.
mono.fallback: ui-monospace, SFMono-Regular, Menlo, Monaco, Courier New

rules: Display headlines use tight letter-spacing; body uses default; eyebrow labels use wide tracking and uppercase.
Headlines are sentence case with a period: 'Make the important work clearer.' not 'MAKE THE IMPORTANT WORK CLEARER' or 'Make The Important Work Clearer'.
Use the heaviest weight only for moments that earn it.
Avoid all-caps body type; reserve uppercase for short eyebrow labels.
No bolding inside paragraphs for emphasis - bolding only for titles, section headers, and labels.
Use en dashes (–), never em dashes (—).
Two type weights only in body content: 400 regular and 500 medium. Never 600 or 700 except in display contexts.

personality.description: Cut through complexity. Help people name what is happening, organize the moving parts, decide what matters next. Direct, structured, plainspoken. Not dumbed down.
The work is strategic, but the thread is people. Warm but not soft. The human side is the work, not a side dish. Avoid HR-brochure or therapy-brand register.
Turn conversation into structure. Airtable, operating models, meeting rhythms, scorecards, frameworks, decks, roadmaps. 'We can actually build this' energy.
Hold tension well. Purpose and performance. Care and accountability. Creativity and discipline. Mature, not simplistic. Not '5 hacks to fix your team.'
Calm, confident, grounded. Calm momentum, not hustle culture. Serious but not stiff. Warm but not sentimental.

voice.perspective: Speak like a trusted advisor, not a vendor. Use 'I help leadership teams...', 'I work with organizations that...', 'Together, we clarify...'. Avoid 'We provide best-in-class solutions...', 'Unlock your potential...', 'Transform your organization...'.

preferences: Use 'follow-through' more than 'execution' when you want warmth

phrases: Turn good intentions into practical systems.
Help teams move from complexity to clarity.
Build the structure around the work.
Create shared language for what matters.
Help leaders have the conversations they have been avoiding.
Make values useful in everyday decisions.
aiTellsToAvoid: No negative parallelism: 'It's not X, it's Y'
No pedagogical voice: 'Let's break this down'
No em dashes - use en dashes (–) or rewrite
No invented concept labels like 'the supervision paradox'

primary.description: 'Nick Banks' set in Inter Black with red dot followed by 'CONSULTING' in Inter Medium uppercase below. The dot is part of the system, not optional.
files: nbc-wordmark.svg (light variant - for white and off-white surfaces, the default)
nbc-wordmark-dark.svg (dark variant - for ink contrast surfaces)

lockup.description: Black NB monogram block to the left of the wordmark. For letterhead, proposal covers, and contexts wanting more presence.
files: nbc-lockup.svg (light variant - for white and off-white surfaces, the default)
nbc-lockup-dark.svg (dark variant - for ink contrast surfaces)

short.description: 'NBC' in Inter Black with red dot tight to the C. For footers, signatures, and tight spaces.
files: nbc-short-mark.svg (light variant - for white and off-white surfaces, the default)
nbc-short-mark-dark.svg (dark variant - for ink contrast surfaces)

monogram.description: Square NB monogram on a black block. Universal mark - works on both ink and off-white surfaces because the block is part of the design and contains its own contrast. Use for favicons, profile pictures, and very small surfaces.

rules: Don't use the wordmark without the red dot on the Consulting line.
Don't recolor outside the palette.
Don't squash, stretch, or rotate the marks.
Don't add effects (shadows, gradients, outlines).
Minimum size for primary wordmark: 120px wide.
Minimum size for short mark: 60px wide.
Minimum size for monogram: 24px square.
Clear space around any mark: at least the height of the 'N' in the wordmark.

redDot.description: A small red circle that lives next to the 'CONSULTING' descriptor on the wordmark, before eyebrow labels on slides, and as a divider in select contexts. The fingerprint of the system.

racingNumbers.description: On section break slides, the section number ('01', '02', '03', '04') appears as an oversized character in brand red at reduced opacity, sitting partially behind or beside the section's headline word. Inspired by racing numbers on rally cars. The number can bleed off the right edge of the surface; cropping is intentional.
racingNumbers.color: #D9352A at 35% opacity on light surfaces, 25% opacity on dark surfaces
racingNumbers.colorNote: The system uses brand red with opacity, not a separate muted color, for system simplicity. There is no 'red-muted' token; the same #D9352A is calibrated to recede via opacity per surface (use --nbc-red-recede-on-light or --nbc-red-recede-on-dark).
racingNumbers.position: Right-aligned, may bleed past the right edge

monoComments.description: JetBrains Mono labels in red appear as inline column headers ('// what we do', '// the tools', '// process'), as captions under section break headlines, and as small annotations. They look like code comments. They signal AI-fluency without bragging.
monoComments.color: #D9352A for labels; #5C5A53 for descriptive captions on white and off-white surfaces; #B8B5AB for descriptive captions on ink surfaces
monoComments.case: lowercase preferred for caption-style mono ('// the part where we ask better questions')
examples: // the actual work, named four ways
// fresh, current, ai-fluent. grounded, human, useful.

redFullBleed.description: One slide or section per artifact gets to be on a fully red surface. Typically the closing slide ('Let's talk.'). The graphic crescendo of the artifact.
redFullBleed.use: Closing slide, hero CTA on web, single editorial moment in long content

redRules.description: 1px red horizontal lines used on the four-pillar layout (top edge of each pillar) and as accent rules on cards. Sparingly.

principles: Type does the work. Graphics support, never lead.
One bold move per surface, with breathing room around it.
Generous whitespace. The eye should always know where to land.
Photography is large, lit like a studio shot, real, never stock.
No decorative chrome: no drop shadows, gradients, rounded soft cards, swooshy graphics, or AI-generated imagery.
Hairline rules at 0.5px for footer separators and section dividers.

deck.format: 16:9 (10" x 5.625"). Default surface is white. Ink variant available for projection in dim rooms.
footerComponents: Hairline rule across page (0.5px, hairlineLight on white, hairlineDark on ink)
Left: NBC short mark with red dot (Inter Black 11pt)
Right: page count in monospace, format '01 / 12' (9pt)

web.contrast: Within a white-led web design, ink (#0E0E0F) sections are used sparingly for emphasis - one or two contrast moments per page. Off-white is NOT used on web; off-white is for documents only.
web.spacing: Generous; sections separated by 4-8rem of vertical space

documents.use: Proposals, contracts, letterhead, printed materials only.
documents.rationale: Documents are read on paper or in PDF readers, where dark surfaces print badly and read poorly. Off-white solves the document use case without dragging the digital brand toward warm/editorial.

composition.eyebrow: Brand name in uppercase ('NICK BANKS CONSULTING')
composition.headline: Tagline in Inter Black 54-72pt, single line, sentence case with period
composition.subtitle: Long tagline in Inter Regular 18pt, max 2 lines

composition.eyebrow: What we believe / Why this matters / etc.
composition.headline: Inter Black 30pt, full argument in 2-3 sentences

slideTypes.name: Section break
slideTypes.purpose: Divides the deck into parts. Adds rhythm.

composition.racingNumber: Oversized section number (01, 02, 03, 04) in brand red at reduced opacity (35% on light surfaces, 25% on dark), right-aligned, may bleed
composition.headlineWord: Single word in Inter Black 130pt ('Why', 'What', 'How', 'Now what')
composition.monoCaption: // the part where [honest framing of what's coming]

slideTypes.name: Portrait + frame
slideTypes.purpose: Human-forward proof. The 'who' of the practice.

rightColumn.image: Real portrait, lit clean, against neutral backdrop
rightColumn.caption: Name in Inter Medium + role in monospace

slideTypes.name: Four pillars
slideTypes.purpose: The work, structured. Clarity, Care, Structure, Momentum.

composition.headline: Inter Black 28pt
composition.fourColumns: Each column: red top rule (1px), mono number (01-04) in red, pillar name in Inter Black 26pt, description in Inter Regular 11pt

composition.headline: Inter Black 28pt
composition.threeCards: Each card: outline rectangle, red top edge (1.2px), mono tag in red ('// healthcare'), title in Inter Black 19pt, description in Inter Regular 11pt

composition.headline: Inter Black 28pt
composition.rows: Each row: mono number (01-04) in red, title in Inter Black 19pt, mono '// honest annotation' to the right, hairline rule between rows

slideTypes.name: Deliverables
slideTypes.purpose: What lands on the client's desk. Tangible.

composition.headline: Inter Black 28pt
composition.twoColumns: Each column: 3 items, each with mono tag in red ('// strategy', '// people'), and Inter Regular 13pt body description

slideTypes.name: Closing - red full bleed
slideTypes.purpose: The graphic crescendo. Call to action.

composition.headline: Short CTA in Inter Black 110pt ('Let's talk.', 'Now what.', etc.)
composition.subtitle: Inter Regular 16pt, one or two sentences
composition.monoContact: Email / phone / website in monospace, separated by ' / '

documentation.footer: Nick Banks Consulting | Victoria, BC | 778.987.5230 | nick@nickbanksconsulting.com | nickbanksconsulting.com

conventions.contracts: Minimal branding, footer only, off-white surface
conventions.workshopMaterials: Match the client's brand, not NBC's
conventions.preferredFormat: Word docs by default, not PDFs, for anything that may need editing

applications.decks: PowerPoint, Keynote, Google Slides at 16:9. White-led by default with red full-bleed closing. Ink variant available for decks shown in dim projection environments.
applications.website: nickbanksconsulting.com - white-led. Ink contrast sections used sparingly for emphasis.
applications.documents: Proposals, letterhead, contracts. Off-white surface, document-appropriate.
applications.social: LinkedIn banner, post images. White-led by default, ink variant for high-impact moments, type-driven.
applications.emailSignature: Lockup or short mark + contact strip in monospace.

surfaceRhythm.principle: Default to white. Use dark and red surfaces when content earns the weight, not on a schedule. Mechanical alternation is not rhythm - it's a pattern. Real rhythm follows the content.
whenToUseWhite: The default. Most content lives here.
Hero/cover sections - open with breathing room
Detailed structured content (process steps, multi-column layouts, lists with annotations)
Reference content like four pillars, deliverables grids, comparison tables
Transitional section breaks that don't need to make a moment
Body-heavy sections where readability matters most
whenToUseInk: The brand's central argument - the manifesto, the 'this is what I believe' moment
Dense card-based layouts where cards have a lot of structure (red top edges, mono tags, titles, descriptions all together) - cards land harder on dark
Section breaks that mark a meaningful transition or pause, especially before the crescendo
Single-statement slides where the goal is gravity, not detail
Quote slides or testimonial moments where you want weight
whenToUseRed: The graphic crescendo - typically the closing slide ('Let's talk.', the CTA)
Never as a transitional surface - red is always the destination, not the journey
antiPatterns: Don't alternate light/dark every section - that's a pattern, not rhythm
Don't use ink for transitional content just to add variety - if the content doesn't earn the weight, stay light
Don't use red surfaces more than once in a single artifact
Don't force the rhythm; let the content drive surface choices
Don't put dense card layouts on white when they'll feel cramped - cards need ink

exampleSequence.description: A typical 12-slide brand deck demonstrating the principle in action. Note this is one possible sequence, not a template - apply judgment to each artifact.

slides.name: Cover
slides.rationale: Open with breathing room, headline does the work

slides.name: Manifesto
slides.rationale: The brand's central argument - earns the weight

slides.name: Section break - Why
slides.rationale: Transitional, no need to make a moment

slides.name: Four pillars
slides.rationale: Structural reference content - reads cleaner on light

slides.name: Recent work cards
slides.rationale: Card density with structure earns dark - cards land harder

slides.name: Section break - Now what
slides.rationale: Final pause before the crescendo - earns weight

slides.name: Closing - Let's talk
slides.rationale: The graphic crescendo - one per artifact
